what: Combine Two Codebooks with Conflict Detection — CombineCodebooks
title: Combine Two Codebooks with Conflict Detection — CombineCodebooks
text: CombineCodebooks compares two codebook data frames (old version new version), identifies added removed variables columns, detects cell--cell differences, produces combined codebook.  records without differences, returns single "Combined" row; records differences, returns "Old" "New" rows, flagged conflicts.

title: Combine Two Codebooks with Conflict Detection — CombineCodebooks
code: CombineCodebooks(OldCodebook, NewCodebook, key = "Variable")

title: Combine Two Codebooks with Conflict Detection — CombineCodebooks
text: OldCodebook data.frame tibble representing old codebook.  row must correspond single variable entry. NewCodebook data.frame tibble representing new codebook.  Must structure (column names) OldCodebook, though extra missing columns handled. key string giving name key column identify variables (e.g. "Variable").  Defaults "Variable".

title: Combine Two Codebooks with Conflict Detection — CombineCodebooks
text: list elements: added_variables: character vector keys present NewCodebook. removed_variables: character vector keys present OldCodebook. columns_added: character vector column names present NewCodebook. columns_removed: character vector column names present OldCodebook. value_differences: tibble cell-level differences (RowID, Field, OldValue, NewValue). combined_df: tibble containing merged codebook versions conflict flag.

title: Combine Two Codebooks with Conflict Detection — CombineCodebooks
text: function: Builds combined_df includes columns inputs: non-conflicting records, single row Version = "Combined". conflicting records, two rows: one Version = "Old" one Version = "New". Flags row CHECKFORCONFLICTS (0 combined, 1 old/new conflict rows).

code: CreateMCATable(   Data,   VarsToReduce,   VariableCategories = NULL,   minThresh = 75,   scale = TRUE,   center = TRUE,   Relabel = TRUE,   Ordinal = FALSE,   numComponents = NULL,   ImputeMissing = FALSE )

title: CreateMCATable — CreateMCATable
text: Data dataframe containing data analyzed. VarsToReduce vector column names Data included MCA. VariableCategories optional vector assign specific categories variables VarsToReduce. used color loadings plot. minThresh numeric value representing minimum cumulative variance threshold determine number components. what: Create normative T-scores from a regression model — CreateNormativeTScores
title: Create normative T-scores from a regression model — CreateNormativeTScores
text: Fits normative regression model user-defined reference subgroup uses model residual standard deviation convert observed scores z-scores T-scores. useful creating demographically adjusted cognitive norms optional practice effect adjustment optional preprocessing unit conversion, log transformation, reverse scoring.

title: Create normative T-scores from a regression model — CreateNormativeTScores
code: CreateNormativeTScores(   df,   test_var,   count_var,   covariates,   reference_var,   reference_value,   include_practice_effect = FALSE,   baseline_count_value = 1,   reverse_score = FALSE,   convert_seconds = FALSE,   seconds_divisor = 1000,   log_transform = TRUE,   codebook = NULL,   return_plots = TRUE )

title: Create normative T-scores from a regression model — CreateNormativeTScores
text: df data frame containing test variable, count variable, reference group variable, covariates. test_var character string naming raw test score variable. count_var character string naming visit count practice count variable. covariates character vector covariate column names include normative model. reference_var character string naming variable used define normative reference group. reference_value value reference_var defines normative reference group. include_practice_effect Logical. TRUE, count_var included predictor model fit using available visits reference group. FALSE, model fit rows count_var == baseline_count_value. baseline_count_value value count_var used define baseline visit include_practice_effect = FALSE. Defaults 1. reverse_score Logical. TRUE, analysis-scale score multiplied -1 higher values reflect better performance. convert_seconds Logical. TRUE, raw score divided seconds_divisor processing. seconds_divisor Numeric divisor used convert_seconds = TRUE. Defaults 1000. log_transform Logical. TRUE, applies log10() analysis score optional unit conversion optional reverse scoring. codebook Optional data frame columns Variable Label. supplied, plot labels use variable labels available. return_plots Logical. TRUE, returns list diagnostic plots.

title: Create normative T-scores from a regression model — CreateNormativeTScores
text: list following elements: data tibble containing original data plus NormRaw, NormScaled, NormPredicted, NormZ, NormT. model fitted lm object. model_summary tibble coefficient estimates. model_fit one-row tibble containing model fit statistics. training_data rows used fit normative model. plots named list ggplot objects return_plots = TRUE. settings list preprocessing modeling settings used.

title: Create normative T-scores from a regression model — CreateNormativeTScores
text: reference group defined reference_var == reference_value. include_practice_effect = FALSE, normative model fit rows count_var == baseline_count_value. include_practice_effect = TRUE, count_var added model available visits reference group used.

title: Detect outliers using the Tukey IQR rule and visualize results — IQROutliers
text: function identifies potential outliers numeric variable using Tukey interquartile range (IQR) rule (Tukey, 1977). returns tibble detected outlier rows ggplot visualization showing variable across groups outlier points highlighted.

title: Detect outliers using the Tukey IQR rule and visualize results — IQROutliers
code: IQROutliers(df, Variable, id = NULL, group = NULL)

title: Detect outliers using the Tukey IQR rule and visualize results — IQROutliers
text: df data frame tibble containing variable evaluate. Variable string specifying name numeric variable test. id string specifying identifier column include returned outlier table. NULL, ID column included returned table. Defaults NULL. group string specifying grouping batch column use x-axis diagnostic plot. NULL, function produce single combined boxplot across rows. Defaults NULL.

title: Detect outliers using the Tukey IQR rule and visualize results — IQROutliers
text: list two elements: outlierdf: tibble containing rows flagged outliers, including ID (requested), variable, group (requested), outlier flag. p: ggplot2 object showing boxplot jittered points colored outlier status.

title: Detect outliers using the Tukey IQR rule and visualize results — IQROutliers
text: outlier rule defined : $$value < Q1 - 1.5 * IQR  \; \textrm{} \;  value > Q3 + 1.5 * IQR$$ Outliers visually highlighted using jittered points colored red, boxplot remains uncolored prevent creation separate outlier-box. color aesthetic mapped within geom_jitter(), ensuring boxplot drawn per group rather per outlier class. Missing values ignored computing quartiles. id group provided must present input data frame.

title: Detect outliers using the Tukey IQR rule and visualize results — IQROutliers
text: Tukey, J. W. (1977). Exploratory Data Analysis. Addison-Wesley.

code: MakeComparisonTable(   DataFrame,   CompVariable = NULL,   Variables,   ...,   Covariates = NULL,   ValueDigits = 2,   pDigits = 3,   AddEffectSize = FALSE,   EffectSizeDigits = 2,   AddPairwise = FALSE,   PairwiseMethod = "bonferroni",   Parametric = TRUE,   ParametricDisplay = NULL,   IncludeOverallN = FALSE,   IncludeMissing = FALSE,   suppress_warnings = FALSE,   Referent = NULL,   IncludeOverallStats = FALSE,   ShowPositiveBinaryOnLabel = TRUE,   CatMethod = c("auto", "chisq", "fisher"),   MultiCatAdjusted = c("multinomial_LR", "none") )

text: DataFrame data frame. CompVariable Grouping variable name (character scalar). NULL missing IncludeOverallStats = TRUE, overall-table returned. Variables Character vector variables include. may also pass additional variable names unnamed arguments via ... (convenience). ... Optional additional variable names (character scalars). Useful accidentally typed Variables="", "B" instead Variables=c("","B"). Covariates Optional covariate names (character vector) used adjusted models. covariates appearing Variables removed displayed table warning. what: SOM + latent profile clustering pipeline (with AHP and distance baselines) — Pipeline_SOMClust
title: SOM + latent profile clustering pipeline (with AHP and distance baselines) — Pipeline_SOMClust
text: End--end pipeline : Standardize variables using SciDataReportR::CalcZScore() supplied Z-score object. Fit Self-Organizing Map (SOM; kohonen) complete cases. Generate aweSOM visualizations (Circular, Line, Cloud) optional relabeling using variable labels original data frame. Cluster SOM codebook vectors using latent profile analysis (tidyLPA / mclust backend). method = "exploratory", fit grid models select recommended solution using Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP)-style index combining AIC, BIC, Entropy. method = "finalize", fit user-specified model number profiles. Map node-level clusters posterior probabilities back individuals. Missing data: SOM clustering fit rows complete Z-scores. returned df_with_clusters full original data .scidr_rowid single cluster column appended; rows used SOM/LPA get NA. Stable row id: .scidr_rowid added input data carried df_with_clusters ProbFit$individual. ProbFit$individual$RowID set equal .scidr_rowid merges rely row order. Z-score behavior: ZScoreType = "Center Scale"/"Center "/"Scale " computes Z-scores df via CalcZScore(). ZScoreType = "ZScoreObj" projects Z-scores using external ZScoreObj via Project_ZScore(). ZScoreType = "PreZScored" uses existing Z-score columns df -re-zscore.
